title: Fix Settings authentication, add 'Other' gender option, and surface change password UI

requirements.text: Fix the Settings page so that authenticated users (logged in via the session auth system) are correctly recognised as authenticated and can use all settings features without seeing an 'not authenticated' or 'login required' error. The Settings page must read auth state from useSessionAuth (not Internet Identity directly) and gate UI only when the session token is genuinely absent.

quotes: when I use something from settings it says I am not authenticated

acceptanceCriteria: A user who has logged in via the Login page can open Settings and see all settings fields without any authentication error message.
Unauthenticated users still see the login prompt on the Settings page.
Changing display name, visibility, language, or gender saves successfully for an authenticated session user.

requirements.text: Add an 'Other' option to the gender selection field on the Settings page, alongside any existing gender options (e.g. Male, Female). The value stored for this option should be 'Other'.

quotes: for gender there should be a "Other" option

acceptanceCriteria: The gender dropdown/selector on the Settings page includes 'Other' as a selectable option.
Selecting 'Other' and saving persists the value correctly.
The backend UserProfile or UserSettings type accepts and stores 'Other' as a valid gender value.

requirements.text: Add a 'Change Password' section to the Settings page. Since the app uses its own session-based username/password authentication (useSessionAuth with localStorage), expose a form that lets the authenticated user enter their current password, a new password, and a confirmation, then updates the stored password in the session auth system. Show a success or error toast on completion.

quotes: I don't see the same things as when I asked to add changing passwords

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Change Password' section is visible on the Settings page for authenticated users.
The form has three fields: Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password.
Submitting with a matching new/confirm password and correct current password updates the password and shows a success toast.
Submitting with an incorrect current password shows an error toast.
Submitting with mismatched new/confirm passwords shows a validation error without calling the update function.

constraints: Do not switch authentication to Internet Identity — the app must continue using the session-based auth system (useSessionAuth) for login, signup, and all auth checks.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All backend Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single actor).
nonGoals: Migrating authentication to Internet Identity or any external auth provider.
Changing the overall Settings page layout beyond the three fixes above.
